summary: Single-filter high-liquidity gate on R6 long is structurally clean with no leakage or integrity issues; only minor advisory monitoring around live overlap and concentration applies.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: novel_dim is solely liquidity_bucket=high, a contemporaneous (T0) liquidity-regime classifier with no forward_*, confirm_after_*, or post-event fields. No future function detected.
risk_assessments.multitest_inflation_notes: R6/Reserved6 channel currently has 35 entries and most use 2+ filters; a single-dim high_liq gate is structurally distinct from the existing multi-filter R6 family, so family-wise FDR contribution is marginal.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Long-side R6 entry could co-fire with the existing 'BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump long' on overlapping symbols in pump regimes, stacking same-side exposure rather than opposing it — paper-shadow monitoring item, not a true conflict.
risk_assessments.concentration_notes: high-liquidity bucket biases triggers toward a small set of top-tier symbols (BTC/ETH/SOL etc.); at 5–10% per trade on $1000 capital, concurrent fills in a pump window could briefly approach effective leverage limits but stays within paper-shadow tolerance.
risk_assessments.adversarial_notes: BWE Reserved6 stream is observable; a participant seeing the signal could front-run, but $1000 notional makes this economically irrelevant and is a generic monitoring concern, not specific to this rule.
risk_assessments.remediation: None required. For paper-shadow tracking only: tag fills with the live OI_Price pump-long strategy to detect same-symbol same-side stacking, and verify liquidity_bucket classifier uses only data available at or before T0.
